'Dhurandhar 2' BO day 28: Ranveer Singh film marches toward Rs. 1100 crore milestone
description: 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge' is redefining box office history with every passing day. Directed by Aditya Dhar and starring Ranveer Singh, Sanjay Dutt, Arjun Rampal, R. Madhavan, Rakesh Bedi and Sara Arjun, the espionage thriller has become the first Hindi film ever to cross Rs. 1,000 crore in India net collections. On day 28, the film is chasing the Rs. 1,100 crore domestic milestone with a live India net of Rs. 1,096.19 crore and a worldwide gross of Rs. 1,727.93 crore.

Bombay high court dismisses Abu Salem’s premature remission plea
description: Bombay High Court dismissed Abu Salem's plea for release, deeming it premature. The court cited a Supreme Court order stating his 25-year sentence, including remission, would conclude in 2030, not now. Salem had claimed he had already served the maximum term agreed upon during his extradition.

Ecstasy decoded: How a ‘party pill’ turned fatal at Mumbai concert
description: Two MBA students' deaths at a Mumbai concert highlight ecstasy's dangers. Known as Molly or E, this party drug, often in colourful pills, can cause fatal overheating and organ strain. Unregulated potency and adulterants, especially when combined with dancing and alcohol, amplify risks, making timely recognition of symptoms crucial.

Meet Yvon Chouinard: The billionaire who gave away his $3 billion company to save the planet
description: Patagonia founder Yvon Chouinard made a groundbreaking move, gifting his $3 billion company to environmental causes.  Instead of personal profit, the company's earnings now fund climate action and conservation. This unique structure, separating control from financial benefit, ensures Patagonia's profits directly support the planet's health, sparking global debate on purpose-driven business.
